summary: Gruber awards TechRadar his 'Jackass of the Week' for a clickbait article claiming Apple urgently needs a foldable iPhone or iPhones won't be worth buying. He dismantles the argument by noting foldables are double-thick, double-expensive, lack dust resistance, and represent only about 1% of global smartphone shipments. He contrasts this with 2013 when Apple genuinely faced consumer demand for larger phones it didn't make, arguing no such demand exists for foldables today. He mocks the original article's claim that competitors could match Apple's refinement but simply choose not to, comparing it to saying other basketball players could play like Michael Jordan but don't bother.
key_points: Current foldables are double-thick and double-expensive ($1,800 for Samsung Fold 5) with no dust resistance, making them a niche product rather than a must-have category
Foldables represent only ~1% of global smartphone shipments and even bullish estimates of 7% in the ultra-premium segment are questionable given actual sales data
Unlike the 2013 large-phone gap where Apple acknowledged 'consumers want what we don't have,' there's no comparable consumer demand signal for foldables today
The vision of super-thin foldable tablets is compelling, but today's technology doesn't deliver on that promise
The original article's logic that competitors choose not to match Apple's refinement is absurd on its face
money_quotes: The promise of a foldable device — pocketable folded, tablet-sized unfolded — is obvious. But the key adjective there is 'super-thin'.
Double-thick foldables strike me as a niche, and today's foldables are double-expensive, not just double-thick.
I would wager heavily there were no 'Consumers want what we don't have' slides at Apple this year regarding foldables.
Foldables — book-style, flip-style, or both — may well become a thing, but they are not yet a thing, because the technology isn't there yet to make them compelling.
Doing more than everything is quite an accomplishment. I'm 110 percent surprised I haven't heard more about this.
Other basketball players could play as well as Michael Jordan, they just don't care to do so. It isn't worth the cost or effort.
key_insight: Foldable phones remain a niche curiosity constrained by physics and price, not a competitive threat Apple needs to urgently address, and clickbait headlines claiming otherwise ignore actual market data.
